# Own Your Code тАФ Stay Here (mac v0 build)

*Generated by `own-your-code`. Disposable тАФ regenerate after any change; never hand-edit.
Certainty tags: **`[code]`** verified in source ┬╖ **`[doc]`** rationale confirmed in `docs/decision_log.md` ┬╖ **`[likely-why]`** reconstructed, verify ┬╖ **`[reconsider]`** judgment.*

## Cockpit

**System (1 paragraph).** Stay Here parses raw DOL OFLC **LCA** disclosure spreadsheets (full **FY2025 Q1тАУQ4**), keeps certified, **entry-wage** (`PW_WAGE_LEVEL = I`) filings for design SOC codes (`15-1255`, `27-1024`, `27-1021`), and emits a grounded shortlist of employers that *actually sponsor entry-wage designers* (`output/sponsors_levelI.csv`), ranked by **repeat-sponsorship across quarters**. A separate **human-reviewed** LLM gap-read (`prompts/gap_read.md`, run in your own Claude/ChatGPT) turns the shortlist + her portfolio + hand-gathered live postings into 3 named-company portfolio projects, slotted into a **private** self-contained HTML report. For an international new-grad designer who needs US visa sponsorship.

**Decisions at a glance.**

| # | Decision | Governing axis | Tier | Your call | Cert |
|---|----------|----------------|------|-----------|------|
| D1 | xlsxтЖТparquet via streaming openpyxl, **by-name** 12-col select | memory vs speed vs deps vs schema-drift | full | тЦв Open | `[code]` |
| D2 | Employer normalization (rule-based, conservative under-merge) | entity-resolution precision/recall | full | тЦв Open | `[code]` |
| D3 | Verification = unit + invariant + golden-killtest asserts | drift-catch vs input-brittleness | full | тЦв **REVISIT** | `[code]` |
| D4 | Engine/altitude split (pure `engine/` package, thin scripts) | reuse + testability + forward-compat | confirm | тШС **OWNED** | `[doc]` |
| D5 | Full-year roll-up + repeat-sponsor ranking | signal strength vs scope | confirm | тШС **OWNED** | `[doc]` |
| D6 | Level-I spelling tolerance + wage annualization | data-vintage robustness | confirm | тШС **OWNED** | `[code]` |
| D7 | Self-contained HTML report (no JS/pandoc) | portability vs deps | confirm | тШС **OWNED** | `[doc]` |
| D8 | Privacy split (private report vs public engine/CSV) | data-exposure boundary | confirm | тШС **OWNED** | `[doc]` |
| D9 | AI confined to human-reviewed gap-read; data step deterministic | trust boundary / grounding | confirm | тШС **OWNED** | `[doc]` |

**Runtime thumbnail.**

```
data/raw/LCA_*_FY2025_Q*.xlsx --convert_quarters (only if no parquet)--> data/processed/lca_fy2025q*.parquet
   --load_certified_rows (filter: Certified тИй design SOC тИй Level-I) --> rows  --build_sponsor_table (groupby EMP_NORM)--> table
   --verify.run_all (raise-and-stop) --> output/sponsors_levelI.csv  (+ _rows.csv = gap-read grounding)
output/sponsors_levelI.csv --build_report--> output/private/stay_here_report.html  (PRIVATE; slots in gap_read_filled.md if present)
engine/ = all logic (NO LLM, no report I/O); scripts/ = thin callers; the LLM gap-read is a manual step outside the engine
```

**Top 5 gaps (where to spend attention).**
1. **xlsx-IO landscape** тАФ openpyxl was the AI default; the calamine/polars/duckdb landscape is unexamined, *and* "streaming" here still materializes all rows in memory (`engine/sponsors.py:159`). [decision + runtime]
2. **Entity-resolution landscape** тАФ conservative rule-based under-merge is a real fork (fuzzy / record-linkage / EIN / alias-map) you haven't placed on its axes. [decision]
3. **`verify_golden_killtest` residual quarter-coupling** тАФ defaults to `IGAVEL` (FY2025 Q4-specific) and isn't gated to a fixture quarter, so a quarters-subset run can fail on correct data. Much smaller than it was, but the last brittle edge. [runtime + decision]
4. **Full-year memory/scaling** тАФ convert + load + `concat` all 4 quarters (one is 144 MB raw); the peak-memory and timing profile at full-year scale is uncharacterized. [runtime]
5. **`requests` is an unused dependency** тАФ in `requirements.txt` with no importer and no download script (downloads are manual). Cruft or an unfinished intent. [hygiene / drift]

<details>
<summary>ЁЯзн Pass 0 тАФ How the system runs (mechanism only)</summary>

*(Mechanism only; the "why" lives in the decision sections.)*

**Module / role**

| file | role | owns |
|------|------|------|
| `engine/sponsors.py` | library (no I/O of reports, no `__main__`) | `ROLE_SOC`, `REQUIRED_COLUMNS`, `xlsx_to_parquet`, `load_certified_rows`, `build_sponsor_table`, `normalize_employer/soc`, wage annualization [D1,D2,D5,D6] |
| `engine/verify.py` | library тАФ the **verification cell** | unit + column + non-empty + count-consistency + collapse + golden-killtest asserts; raises `VerificationError` [D3] |
| `engine/__init__.py` | package surface | re-exports the engine API |
| `scripts/convert_quarters.py` | entrypoint тАФ **run 1st** | discovers `data/raw/*.xlsx`, converts each missing тЖТ parquet |
| `scripts/build_shortlist.py` | entrypoint тАФ **run 2nd** | provenance + engine + `verify.run_all` (gate) + writes the CSVs |
| `scripts/build_report.py` | entrypoint тАФ **run 3rd** | renders the private HTML report from the CSV (+ gap-read if present) |
| `prompts/gap_read.md` | **human step** | the reviewed LLM gap-read, run in your own Claude/ChatGPT |
| `docs/decision_log.md` | the Judgment Ledger | the forks + why (your design source-of-truth) |

**Control + data flow.** `engine` holds all logic; scripts are thin callers. `convert_quarters` writes a parquet per quarter **only if it doesn't already exist** (`scripts/convert_quarters.py:39`, `--force` to redo). `build_shortlist` calls `load_certified_rows` (reads parquet only; raises `FileNotFoundError` if a quarter's parquet is missing) тЖТ `build_sponsor_table` (groups certified rows by `EMP_NORM`) тЖТ `verify.run_all(rows, table)` which **raises and stops** on the first failed check тЖТ writes `sponsors_levelI.csv` (one row/employer) + `sponsors_levelI_rows.csv` (row-level grounding for the gap-read). `build_report` reads only the CSV (+ optional `output/private/gap_read_filled.md`). The LLM gap-read touches none of the pipeline тАФ it's a manual step whose output is slotted into the report.

**Cold-start sequence (empty clone тЖТ report).**

```bash
python3 -m venv .venv
.venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt
# Download DOL LCA quarters into data/raw/ named LCA_Disclosure_Data_FY2025_Q1.xlsx тАж Q4.xlsx
#   (from dol.gov OFLC performance-data page; ~80тАУ144 MB each)
.venv/bin/python scripts/convert_quarters.py      # raw xlsx -> data/processed/*.parquet (only missing; --force to redo)
.venv/bin/python scripts/build_shortlist.py       # verify (gate) + write output/sponsors_levelI.csv (+ _rows.csv)
.venv/bin/python scripts/build_report.py          # -> output/private/stay_here_report.html
# MANUAL gap-read: fill the 4 blocks in prompts/gap_read.md, run in your own Claude/ChatGPT,
#   review every named-company claim, save to output/private/gap_read_filled.md, rerun build_report.py
```

**Prereqs / env.** Python 3.12 (per `__pycache__`) ┬╖ `pandas`, `pyarrow`, `openpyxl` (`requests` is listed but unused) ┬╖ **no external binary** (no `/browse` dependency). Raw xlsx are large and gitignored.

**Inputs.** DOL OFLC LCA disclosure xlsx тЖТ `data/raw/LCA_Disclosure_Data_FY2025_Q<n>.xlsx`. The filename must match `FILE_RE` in `convert_quarters.py:22` (`LCA_Disclosure_Data_<FY####>_<Q#>.xlsx`) or it's skipped. Role/quarters are CLI args to `build_shortlist.py` (defaults: `--role design`, all four FY2025 quarters).

**Outputs / artifacts.** `data/processed/lca_fy2025q*.parquet` (cache, consumed by the engine) ┬╖ `output/sponsors_levelI.csv` (public shortlist тЖТ consumed by `build_report`) ┬╖ `output/sponsors_levelI_rows.csv` (row-level grounding тЖТ consumed by the human gap-read) ┬╖ `output/private/stay_here_report.html` (**private**) ┬╖ `output/private/gap_read_filled.md` (human-authored тЖТ consumed by `build_report`).

**"Is it working?" signals.** convert тЖТ `converted FY2025Q4: N rows -> тАж` (or `exists, skip`). build_shortlist тЖТ a `PROVENANCE` block with every quarter `[OK]`, a `VERIFICATION CELL` of `PASS тАФ` lines, then `RESULT`: `distinct sponsor employers : M` and a real Top-20 (Amazon/Deloitte/Activision/Esri, not staffing-dominated) = **GO**; any `VerificationError` raises and stops = **NO-GO**. build_report тЖТ `wrote output/private/stay_here_report.html (M companies, R repeat sponsors)`.

**Human-in-the-loop.** (1) The **gap-read** тАФ run the prompt in your own LLM, review every named-company claim before it reaches her. (2) The **OPT-now eyeball** тАФ manually check each pointed-to company for a live entry-level posting (LCA = "sponsors," not "hiring now"). (3) **Privacy** тАФ never publish anything under `output/private/`.

**Failure / debug.** `No raw files in data/raw/` тЖТ download a DOL quarter. `missing required columns [тАж]. Wrong file (PERM not LCA?)` тЖТ wrong/renamed file. `skip (unrecognized name)` тЖТ filename doesn't match `FILE_RE`. `FileNotFoundError: Missing parquet for FY2025Qx` тЖТ run `convert_quarters` first. `VerificationError`: normalization-unit fail тЖТ `normalize_employer` regression; non-empty fail тЖТ filter broke / wrong Level-I spelling for the vintage; **golden-killtest fail тЖТ either real parse drift OR you ran quarters that don't contain `IGAVEL`** (see D3). Stale/odd output тЖТ delete `data/processed/*.parquet` and re-`convert --force`.

**Drive to a new case.** New role = add a `ROLE_SOC` entry + `SOC_TITLES` (one line) then `build_shortlist.py --role тАж`. New quarter = drop the correctly-named xlsx in `data/raw/`, `convert_quarters`, then `build_shortlist --quarters FY2025Q3,тАж`. Be aware: `build_shortlist` runs the golden-killtest with its default `IGAVEL` regardless of which quarters you pass.

**Runtime state.** `data/processed/*.parquet` persists between runs (delete or `--force` to invalidate). `output/*` is regenerated every run. `output/private/gap_read_filled.md` is human-authored and persists; `build_report` slots it in if present, else shows a placeholder.
</details>

## Decisions (depth)

<details>
<summary>D1 тАФ xlsx тЖТ parquet via streaming openpyxl, by-name 12-col select ┬╖ Open ┬╖ full</summary>

1. **The decision.** `engine/sponsors.py:134` `xlsx_to_parquet`: open the 100+ MB raw xlsx with `openpyxl(read_only=True)`, read the header row, **resolve the 12 `REQUIRED_COLUMNS` by name** (not index), pull only those columns, coerce wages to numeric / everything else to string, write a narrow parquet. Downstream reads parquet only. `[code]`
2. **Why it works that way.** `read_only=True` uses openpyxl's streaming reader (rows yielded lazily, the whole workbook XML is never built into objects). By-name resolution survives the fact that **column order is not stable across quarters** (the comment + `[doc]` B4 note a real index bug this caught: FY2025 Q1 has `WORKSITE_COUNTY` where Q4 has `WORKSITE_CITY`). Parquet then gives typed, columnar, fast repeat reads so the slow xlsx parse happens once per quarter.
3. **Decision axes.** peak memory ┬╖ cold-read speed ┬╖ dependency weight/boringness ┬╖ robustness to schema drift (column reorder/rename) ┬╖ column pushdown.
4. **Option landscape.**

   | option | memory | speed | deps | schema-drift | notes |
   |--------|--------|-------|------|--------------|-------|
   | `pandas.read_excel` | тЬЧ high (loads all) | slow | pandas | by-name ok | OOM risk on 144 MB |
   | **stream openpyxl (chosen)** | ~ok | slow-ish | boring | by-name тЬУ | rows still materialized (see gap) |
   | `python-calamine` | low | **fast** | newer dep | by-name тЬУ | Rust reader; big speedup |
   | `polars.read_excel` | low | fast | new dep | тЬУ | also calamine-backed |
   | LibreOffice тЖТ CSV | low | manual | external | n/a | ops burden |
   | `duckdb` (spatial/excel) | low | fast | new dep | query | better for the v2 query path |
5. **Why this wins here.** A handful of one-off quarterly parses тЖТ "low memory + boring, already-needed deps + robust to DOL's column churn" beats raw speed. `openpyxl` is already implied by the xlsx world; no new runtime. `[likely-why]`
6. **Rebuild path.** Read one 100 MB LCA quarter three ways тАФ `read_excel`, stream-openpyxl, `python-calamine` тАФ and record peak RSS + wall time for each. Then re-read only 12 columns via calamine and feel the speedup. You'll own *why* openpyxl is the safe-not-fast pick and exactly what you'd trade to switch.
7. **Firsthand docs.** openpyxl "Read-only / optimised modes"; pandas IO (`read_excel`); python-calamine README; Apache Parquet + `pyarrow` docs.
8. **Would I make this call again?** Yes for v0. `[reconsider]` Two things: (a) `rows = [tuple(r[i] for i in idx) for r in ws.iter_rows(...)]` (`:159`) materializes **every** row into a Python list before the DataFrame тАФ it streams the *parse* but not memory; for the 144 MB quarter, profile it. (b) For the v2 automated quarterly refresh, swap to calamine/polars + duckdb.
</details>

<details>
<summary>D2 тАФ Employer normalization (rule-based, conservative under-merge) ┬╖ Open ┬╖ full</summary>

1. **The decision.** `engine/sponsors.py:94` `normalize_employer`: uppercase тЖТ strip punctuation тЖТ drop trailing corporate suffixes (`LLC/INC/CORP/LTD/тАж`) тЖТ drop a leading `THE`. `None` for blank names (so they group and get excluded). Grouping in `build_sponsor_table` is on this `EMP_NORM`. `[code]`
2. **Why it works that way.** Collapses spelling/entity variants ("The Deloitte Consulting, LLP." and "Deloitte Consulting LLP" тЖТ `DELOITTE CONSULTING`) so one employer is one row and the filing count is real. Deliberately **conservative** тАФ it will *not* merge genuinely different legal spellings (`[doc]` known-limits): over-merging two real companies is the worse error than splitting one.
3. **Decision axes.** recall (catch all variants) ┬╖ precision (don't merge Apple Inc / Apple Bank) ┬╖ explainability ┬╖ maintenance ┬╖ over- vs under-merge bias.
4. **Option landscape.** exact match (splinters variants) ┬╖ **rule-based suffix strip (chosen)** (precise, explainable, misses `IBM`тЖФ`International Business Machines`) ┬╖ fuzzy/`rapidfuzz` (catches more, risks false merges) ┬╖ record-linkage/`dedupe` (overkill at this scale) ┬╖ canonical key (EIN / DUNS) ┬╖ small alias map (cheap recall boost).
5. **Why this wins here.** The shortlist is ~tensтАУlow-hundreds of employers you can eyeball, and it underwrites a person's career bet тЖТ precision + explainability > recall. A false merge would mislead her; a missed variant just splits a count slightly. `[doc]`
6. **Rebuild path.** Normalize 30 real `EMPLOYER_NAME` values by rule; then run `rapidfuzz` at ratio 90 and 80 and watch a false merge appear. Then add a 5-entry alias map and see recall rise without the false-merge risk. You'll own the precision/recall dial.
7. **Firsthand docs.** `rapidfuzz` docs; `dedupe` / `recordlinkage` docs; the SOC/employer fields in the DOL LCA record layout.
8. **Would I make this call again?** Yes for v0 at this scale. `[reconsider]` Add a small curated alias map before reaching for fuzzy; revisit only if the list grows past eyeball size or feeds an automated product.
</details>

<details>
<summary>тЪая╕П D3 тАФ Verification = unit + invariant + golden-killtest asserts ┬╖ REVISIT ┬╖ full</summary>

1. **The decision.** `engine/verify.py` `run_all`: a "verification cell" that **raises `VerificationError` and stops the run** on any failure. Six checks: `verify_normalization_unit` (data-independent), `verify_columns`, `verify_nonempty`, `verify_count_consistency` (╬г filing_count == selected rows), `verify_collapse` (a multi-spelling employer collapses to one row, else "grouping reduced rows"), `verify_golden_killtest` (top kill-test sponsor `IGAVEL` present). `[code]`
2. **Why it works that way.** A wrong file (PERM not LCA), a broken filter, or a normalization regression can never silently produce a bogus shortlist that someone bets a career on. Most checks are **quarter-independent invariants** (units, schema, count-consistency, collapse) тАФ they hold for *any* quarter тАФ so this build largely fixed the prior version's quarter-brittleness.
3. **Decision axes.** bug-class caught ┬╖ brittleness to correct input change ┬╖ maintenance cost ┬╖ suitability for CI ┬╖ coverage vs cost.
4. **Option landscape.** no checks ┬╖ ad-hoc prints ┬╖ **invariant asserts + one golden anchor (chosen)** ┬╖ `pytest` suite ┬╖ schema validation (`pandera`) ┬╖ property-based (`hypothesis`). The chosen mix is invariant-heavy (robust) with a single golden anchor (sharp drift detector).
5. **Why this wins here.** Inline raise-and-stop = a notebook-style gate with zero framework, and the invariant checks travel across quarters. The golden anchor catches "the parse silently drifted" that pure schema checks miss. `[doc]` (B4: an assert caught the real index bug mid-build.)
6. **Rebuild path.** Add a `pandera` schema for the parquet and a `hypothesis` test that `normalize_employer` is idempotent. Then make `verify_golden_killtest` take a **fixture quarter** (assert the anchor only when that quarter is in scope) and feel the difference between "input changed" and "parse drifted."
7. **Firsthand docs.** `pandas.testing`; `pandera` schema docs; `hypothesis` docs.
8. **Would I make this call again?** Mostly yes тАФ this is a strong design. `[reconsider]` **The residual:** `verify_golden_killtest` defaults to `IGAVEL` (an FY2025 Q4 employer) and `build_shortlist` runs it against *whatever* `--quarters` you pass, with no override. Run a quarters-subset without iGavel and it raises on correct data тАФ the prior build's exact failure mode, now shrunk to one of six checks. **Fix:** gate the golden anchor to a committed fixture quarter; let arbitrary quarters pass on invariants + schema only.
</details>

<details>
<summary>D4 тАФ Engine/altitude split (pure engine package, thin scripts) ┬╖ OWNED ┬╖ confirm</summary>

`engine/` holds all logic and imports nothing about HTML/PDF/reports; `scripts/*` are thin callers. This is `decision_log` #6 (the Level-I parse *is* the engine a future self-serve UI calls) and it's faithfully built тАФ `build_sponsor_table` is role-parameterized and report-agnostic. `[doc]` Nit: the four default quarters live in `build_shortlist.py:30` while paths/columns live in the engine; if more constants spread across scripts, lift them to one config.
</details>

<details>
<summary>D5 тАФ Full-year roll-up + repeat-sponsor ranking ┬╖ OWNED ┬╖ confirm</summary>

`build_sponsor_table` sorts by `quarters_present` (distinct quarters an employer appears in) first, then `filing_count`. The product claim "*actually* sponsors entry-wage designers" is far stronger for a company seen across multiple quarters than one with a single spike. This is `decision_log` B2 тАФ and because the engine is quarter-parameterized, full-year was a one-call extension, not new scope (single quarter remains the documented kill-test). `[doc]` Governing axis: signal strength vs scope creep тАФ owned.
</details>

<details>
<summary>D6 тАФ Level-I spelling tolerance + wage annualization ┬╖ OWNED ┬╖ confirm</summary>

`LEVEL_I_VALUES = {"I","LEVEL I","1"}` (`engine/sponsors.py:66`) tolerates how `PW_WAGE_LEVEL` is spelled across DOL vintages; the comment tells you to `value_counts()` before trusting a new vintage (FY2025 uses bare `"I"`). `_annualized_wage` multiplies `WAGE_RATE_OF_PAY_FROM` by a unit factor so wages compare across HOUR/YEAR/etc. тАФ explicitly a *secondary* display signal, not a filter. `[code]` Both are small, data-aware robustness calls; owned. Watch: an unknown wage unit silently yields `None` (acceptable, since wage is secondary).
</details>

<details>
<summary>D7 тАФ Self-contained HTML report (no JS/pandoc) ┬╖ OWNED ┬╖ confirm</summary>

`build_report.py` emits one HTML file with embedded CSS, no JS, no external assets, via f-strings + a minimal hand-rolled `md_to_basic_html` for the gap-read block. `decision_log` B7: pandoc isn't installed and a single self-contained file opens anywhere and is trivially shareable for early UX feedback; PDF stays optional. `[doc]` Owned. Watch: `md_to_basic_html` is intentionally minimal (headings/bullets/bold only) тАФ fine for the controlled gap-read input, not general markdown.
</details>

<details>
<summary>D8 тАФ Privacy split (private report vs public engine/CSV) ┬╖ OWNED ┬╖ confirm</summary>

Her portfolio-derived report goes to `output/private/` (gitignored); the engine, method, decision log, and the **public-record** LCA shortlist CSV are shareable. `decision_log` B6. `[doc]` This is the data-exposure boundary, and it's the right one тАФ LCA employer names are public record; her job-search/portfolio data is not. Owned. The boundary lives in `.gitignore` + the "Private" footer in the report; if this ever goes multi-user, the boundary needs to be enforced in code, not convention.
</details>

<details>
<summary>D9 тАФ AI confined to human-reviewed gap-read; data step deterministic ┬╖ OWNED ┬╖ confirm</summary>

The engine is **NO-LLM** and deterministic; the only AI is the gap-read (`prompts/gap_read.md`), run in your own LLM with you (Noah) as the reviewer before anything reaches her. `decision_log` #5: the data step must be traceable, so AI on the parse would inject ungrounded error into the moat (grounding). `[doc]` This is the trust spine of the product and it's owned. Watch: the guarantee is *convention* (a prompt file + "you review"), not enforced тАФ if a future version automates the gap-read, the human-review boundary disappears and the grounding claim weakens.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Drift тАФ built vs docs/decision_log.md</summary>

**Faithful to the ledger (this build is unusually clean):** advisory diagnostic, not a directory (#1) тЬУ ┬╖ `PW_WAGE_LEVEL=I` signal (#2) тЬУ ┬╖ raw LCA source (#3) тЬУ ┬╖ designer ICP via `ROLE_SOC` (#4, #7) тЬУ ┬╖ AI judgment-only (#5) тЬУ ┬╖ engine/altitude split (#6) тЬУ ┬╖ **Layer A (OPT-now postings pipeline) cut to v2** (#8) тЬУ ┬╖ full-year roll-up (B2) тЬУ ┬╖ FY2025 + bare-`"I"` (B3) тЬУ ┬╖ by-name columns (B4) тЬУ ┬╖ module+script not notebook (B5) тЬУ ┬╖ privacy split (B6) тЬУ ┬╖ self-contained HTML (B7) тЬУ.

**The headline:** unlike the earlier build, there is **no `ingest_portfolio.py` / no automated scraper** тАФ the portfolio input is manual via `gap_read.md`, exactly as the ledger's Layer-A cut and Layer-3 "portfolio as text/links" intended. The prior build's unplanned 242-line scraper drift is **absent here.** This build *matches its design.*

**Minor drift / cruft to own:**
- **`requests>=2.31` is in `requirements.txt` but never imported** тАФ no download script exists (downloads are manual per the README). Either wire a fetch helper or drop the dep. Smells like an unfinished "auto-download quarters" intent.
- **No standalone kill-test entrypoint.** The ledger's B1 kill-test ("run one quarter first, is the door open?") is now folded into `build_shortlist`'s provenance + `verify_golden_killtest` rather than a separate `feasibility_check` script. Defensible (the ledger frames B1 as a one-time manual run), but there's no longer a single GO/NO-GO command тАФ the GO/NO-GO is implicit in reading `build_shortlist` output.

**Open tension (carried, not a bug):** the golden-killtest anchor (`IGAVEL`, FY2025 Q4) vs "run any quarter/subset" тАФ the ledger doesn't address how golden checks behave on arbitrary quarters (see D3).
</details>

<details>
<summary>Gaps ranked (both axes)</summary>

1. **xlsx-IO landscape + "streaming" memory reality** [decision + runtime] тАФ the most AI-defaulted call and the engine's foundation; also `:159` materializes all rows (profile it). Biggest re-derivation gap and matters most for the v2 automation goal.
2. **Entity-resolution landscape** [decision] тАФ the conservative under-merge is defensible but its alternatives (fuzzy / record-linkage / EIN / alias-map) are unplaced. One toy build closes it.
3. **`verify_golden_killtest` residual quarter-coupling** [runtime + decision] тАФ small, well-understood; gate the anchor to a fixture quarter. (This is the *shrunken* descendant of the prior build's #1 bug.)
4. **Full-year memory/scaling** [runtime] тАФ convert + load + concat 4 quarters (one 144 MB raw); the peak-memory/timing profile is uncharacterized. If it ever runs in CI or on her laptop, you need to know.
5. **`requests` unused dep / no download automation** [hygiene / drift] тАФ remove it or finish the intent; today it's a dependency that does nothing.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Drills</summary>

**Decision self-interview (cold).** Three ways to read a 100 MB xlsx and when each wins ┬╖ why parquet over CSV for the interchange ┬╖ merge `Amazon.com Services LLC` and `AMAZON.COM SERVICES, INC.` without merging Apple Inc / Apple Bank ┬╖ which `verify` checks survive a new quarter and which one doesn't, and why ┬╖ what makes `quarters_present` a stronger signal than `filing_count` ┬╖ why the engine is NO-LLM and what would break if AI touched the parse ┬╖ where the public/private boundary lives and how it would have to change for multi-user.

**Runtime drill.** Trace one `EMPLOYER_NAME` cell from a raw xlsx row тЖТ `xlsx_to_parquet` тЖТ `load_certified_rows` (filter) тЖТ `normalize_employer` тЖТ `groupby("EMP_NORM")` тЖТ a row in `sponsors_levelI.csv`, naming each function and file it passes through. Then: *"`build_shortlist` just raised `VerificationError` on `verify_golden_killtest` тАФ is that a real parse drift or did I run the wrong quarters? How do I tell in 30 seconds?"*

**Rebuild checklist.** (a) Read one LCA quarter via `read_excel` vs stream-openpyxl vs `python-calamine`, record peak RSS + time. (b) Rule-normalize 30 employer names, then `rapidfuzz` at 90/80 and find a false merge. (c) Add a `pandera` schema + a `hypothesis` idempotence test for `normalize_employer`. (d) Refactor `verify_golden_killtest` to gate on a committed fixture quarter; let arbitrary quarters pass on invariants only. (e) Profile `build_shortlist` across all 4 quarters; note peak memory. (f) Remove `requests` (or add the download helper it implies) and re-run end-to-end on a fresh quarter.
</details>
